doc.title: Cash now vs. on account — same event, different second line
doc.event: Two versions of the same week: the studio earns $1,500 of revenue and incurs $600 of advertising. In version one everything settles in cash; in version two everything is on account. Journalize both worlds.

lines.label: asset in
lines.trap: Coincidence, not a rule — the revenue credit is identical either way.

lines.label: earned
lines.why: Recognized because the work is done.
lines.trap: The credit never changes across the axis — flip it and watch.

lines.label: cost used up
lines.trap: The debit never changes across the axis either.

lines.label: paid now
lines.trap: Only THIS line differs between worlds.

lines.label: right to collect
lines.why: The asset is a claim instead of cash — value either way.
lines.trap: Revenue timing did NOT move. Accrual accounting recognizes earning, not collecting.

lines.label: earned — identical
lines.why: Same credit as the cash world.
lines.trap: If your revenue changes when payment timing changes, you're doing cash-basis by accident.

entries.caption: Receive the $600 ad bill, pay later

lines.label: cost used up — identical
lines.why: The ads ran; the cost is incurred.
lines.trap: Expense doesn't wait for payment any more than revenue waits for collection.

lines.label: owe it instead
lines.trap: Later payment (Dr AP / Cr Cash) touches NO expense — the cost was already counted. Double-counting it is the classic follow-up error.

memorize.body: Payment timing changes ONE line: the cash line becomes AR (if owed to you) or AP (if you owe). The revenue/expense line is untouchable — earning and incurring set it, not cash.
Settling later is a two-entry story: recognize now (revenue/expense + AR/AP), settle later (Cash + AR/AP). The second entry never repeats the income effect.

questions.prompt: Last month's $600 ad bill (already recorded) is paid today. Today's entry debits…

distractors.feedback: Debiting Advertising Expense again would count the cost twice. It was recognized when incurred; today is just Dr Accounts Payable 600 / Cr Cash 600.

build.scaffold: Write the income line first (it never changes), then ask: did cash move? If not, AR or AP takes its seat.
